But before we do, I think it is important that we actually explore offline marketing. I mean, offline marketing is of course the genesis to online marketing. You just want was before. It's the way we've done things before. Now, let me just explain quickly what direct response marketing is. I know a lot of you guys are already familiar with the concept, but for those of you who direct response marketing, it's a powerful form of marketing that is all results based. So we know whether or not we done well because we can actually track our results. So what that means is a direct response campaign would be one where we asked our audience, we put a message to the market and that message encouraged our audience to take a particular action. So for instance, that could be calling a hotline. It could be leaving some information on a website. It could be making a purchase directly. But we're encouraging somebody to do something in the message of our campaign. Okay. Now that is distinct and different from branded marketing. Now, be mindful of branded campaigns that don't encourage you to do anything. I mean, the most popular ones, I think we, no matter whereabouts in the world you are, you'd agree that you're likely to have been exposed to Coca-Cola branding adverts where you see a few good, happy, healthy looking people drinking a bottle of Coke at the beach and enjoying themselves with no actually caught to action. So there's, you don't see anything on the billboard that encourages you or that television commercial that encourages you to go right away and either purchase a bottle of Coke or send a message to Coca-Cola head offices in exchange for getting a report on why Coca-Cola is so much better than a competitors, whatever it is. So we have branded campaigns, which are predominantly or even exclusively designed to create awareness of a particular product or offering. And then of course, we have direct response campaigns. Direct response campaign, which is what we're primarily concerned with, I get towards actually putting people through a sales funnel. And we won't go into the details or the nuances of a sales funnel just yet. We will explore it a little later in this module and we'll cover it ad nauseam in another module. But just for now, a sales funnel is a little more than a sales sequence. So we want somebody to take one particular action. It could be something as simple as having them submit their information on an opt-in form. Okay, so we could have a form online that says putting your email address and you'll be sent a report on whatever. So that's it. We've encouraged them to take a particular action. So that's what the direct response campaign is all about. And we're familiar with direct response campaigns and gated content. Or since we're all online, we're all familiar with that. But this was actually has its genesis or has its beginning in the offline marketing world. I mean, the offline marketing world actually gave birth to direct response marketing. And this is how it was done before. So in the past, what we were given was an advert, whether it was on the radio or it could have been in a newspaper article. And we were encouraged to call a toll free number in exchange for a valuable piece of content, something useful for us. So it could have been something as simple as call a particular number, 1-300 number in exchange for a report on the fastest and safest way to lose six kilos in a week and a half without dieting or exercise. Okay, so so then we have our first icon, which is a phone call and that indicates that we were encouraged to make a call. We would go ahead and make a call and then we would be sent the report via physical report in the past that would be mailed to our address. And what was on that report? Well, they would have to deliver on their promise. In that particular case, it was the six or seven ways on how we were expected to lose weight without diet and exercise. But really that formed only part of the report that was sent. And the majority of the report was actually a sales letter encouraging readers to actually make a high ticket purchase or make any kind of purchase, frankly. So we were actually, so let's run through that process again. We read the newspaper, we were encouraged to make a phone call. We made the phone call in exchange for a particular report that we were really eager to receive. We were sent the report. The report gave us a little bit of content, but it didn't give us all of it. We kept some secrets and it encouraged us to pay and make a purchase for a particular product in exchange for the complete information. So what we were sent was a report that was useful, but incomplete. And it was presented in the form of a sales letter. Then we were encouraged to make a purchase. So that's the offline method. And that truly was the birth of modern day sales funnels. So we had all started off so simply, advert, phone call, report. Report was actually a disguise sales letter and the sales letter encouraged us to make a purchase. There you go. Sales funnels. That's how it all began. It all started that way. So let's take a look at it in live action. All right. So this is Jordan's opt-in page. So you could see this virtually anywhere. So say hypothetically, you will run a particular blog, a relevant blog that we purchase traffic on. So say hypothetically, you were on the Wall Street Journal. You're looking around on the Wall Street Journal and on the right-hand side, we had purchased a banner ad that said something like get access or have watched this exclusive interview with Jordan Belford, where he explains the art of persuasion. Okay. So you click that banner and then you're going to end up on this page, which is of course our opt-in page, otherwise known as a lead page, otherwise known as a squeeze page. Okay. So in this case, we call them an opt-in page. So you're on an opt-in page and what does an opt-in page do? It encourages the visitor to leave their details. That's it. That's the objective of an opt-in page. We just want their details. You have an option to leave your name. Alternatively, you can just leave your email address in some markets. We've tested this pretty aggressively in some markets. Conversion rates are far higher when requesting visitors to leave only their email address that we've decided to scrap the name. So if this was the case in this particular opt-in page, you wouldn't see that bracket there. It's encouraging visitors to leave that element, which encouraging visitors to leave their name. That would have been scrapped and we will just have an email address. In this particular market, it made sense to have a name so we could personalize messages. So encourage visitors to leave their name, leave their email address and submit. Now, there are a few interesting things on this opt-in page that I want everybody to be mindful of when you're creating your own opt-in pages. And we'll go through how to do all this in the future. But again, big picture stuff here. We need a headline. The headline needs to speak straight to the point. In this particular case, we've said you've seen the movie, now meet the real Wolf of Wall Street. Okay, so everybody knows or whoever's clicked on the particular advert knows or intends to watch an interview or read or watch an interview about with Jordan Goldford explaining his secrets to the art of persuasion and to build massive wealth and to bounce back from devastating setbacks. So we listed headline that lets the visitor know they're at the right place. Okay, so there are different strategies to crafting your headline. So we're going to go through this in the copyrighting course. But in this particular case, we just want to let the visitors know that you want to Jordan Goldford, you want access to the real Wolf of Wall Street. Here it is. And we put that in the headline so they know they're in the right place. And then we wanted to provide some kind of a tease with respect to the actual gated content. And by gated content, I mean the content that is of course behind the gate. In this particular case, the gate is the opt-in will require an admin email address. If you submit your name in the email address, you will have access to this particular video. So we put a little video teaser there. If you click that link where you see that play button, nothing happens. In some cases, I would encourage clients to click the link and have another opt-in box show up. In this particular case, it was fine for us just to have that play button unclickable. So nothing happened. It's just a regular image there. And then you have some benefit bullets, some benefit copy. So what's in the video? What's the video going to do for them? What are they going to have access to after they watch the video? The value of watching the video and so forth. In this particular case, it was an exclusive interview. It would show Jordan will show his secrets on how to allow visitors or viewers to bounce back from devastating setbacks. So it goes into or delves into Jordan's a secret for resilience or his strategies to become more resilient. He'd speak or share some of the strategies and tips behind the art of persuasion and how anyone can build massive wealth. But as you guys all know by now, after you clicked or you submit for a free report, are you really given all the information or are you given useful but incomplete information? I'm sure you're all on the other end now saying useful but incomplete. So Jordan's going to teach you how to build massive wealth. He's going to teach you the art of persuasion. He is going to teach you some of his skills and strategies for dealing with setbacks and becoming more resilient. But he's not going to give you the whole thing. Is he? You're going to have a great interview, but it's going to be incomplete. And if you want the complete system on how to accomplish any of all of those benefits, you'll have to move on to the next step or stage in our sales funnel. So that's an opt-in page or a lead page or a squeeze page. Now let's take a look at a lead gen page. Now, here's the difference between a lead gen page and a squeeze page. It's as simple as a lead gen page just asks for more information. It's as simple as that. In this particular case, what's the lead magnet? What's the vape? So a free quote, you know, it can be as simple as that. It doesn't need to be a report. It doesn't need to be a physical product. The only requirement is that it's something that the visitor wants. In this particular case, we know people were searching for moving moving and storage facilities or a removalist. So we know people are looking for a particular removalist and they want the and they want to quote. So what we're going to tell them, we're going to offer them a free quote in exchange for their details. But in this case, this is not an opt-in page per se, but rather a lead generation page. The difference simply is that a lead gen page elicits enough information so that it can be passed on to a salesperson and they can then convert the prospect onto the next stage of our sales funnel or the next stage of our sequence. And you're all familiar with the ideas of sales funnels and sequences by now. So what's the difference? Simple. A lead gen page wants phone numbers. It often wants address. It wants added information. In this particular case, it's asking for the moving date, the date you want the equipment or furniture move and just asking for your zip code. So just wants to know whereabouts you are. So it's as simple as that. So we have a lead page or an opt-in page that only wants the email address. And in most cases, a name coupled with the email address, but of course in some cases, we don't even need a name. And then we have a lead gen page, which requires a little bit more information because that lead is actually going to be passed on to a physical salesperson who is expected to convert that lead and run them through the next stage of the sales sequence or the next stage of the sales funnel. So it's very similar to a very similar but different in the amount of information they plan on eliciting from the visitor once they first land on the page. Now the next one is a sales letter. In this case, let's stick with our original analogy where we had somebody wanting to or we had a visitor wanting to lose weight. They've opted in, they've submitted their details and now they're sent a particular report. Okay, so they'll send a report on the six or seven ways to lose six or seven pounds or kilos, whatever it was, without dieting or exercise. So this would be that report. Okay, in this particular case, we're looking at one of Jordan's sales letters. So somebody's opted in. For instance, they could have opted into that last video interview we've sent. Rather than having the video play on the next page, in this particular case, we would have put a little message up the top that would say or instruct the visitors that the particular interview had been emailed to their inbox. Allow five or 10 minutes for delivery. In the meantime, we know you're interested in Jordan. We know you're confident with Jordan's persuasive abilities and his abilities to train and motivate and get people confident with respect to their own skills and their own abilities and so forth. So in light of all of that, we think that you might be interested in this and then we'll send them to a sales letter, which is just a page on the website that encourages a particular purchase. So in this case, it is a sales letter that's encouraging visitors to purchase or at least consider the purchase of the straight line persuasion system, which is the SLP system. And then we have a compelling headline, a compelling subhead and a great lead. And we'll discuss all these little elements of a sales letter on how to create a persuasive sales letter in a future module, in the copywriting module, namely. But there you have it. That's a sales letter. Okay, so now we know exactly how you guys as students who have enrolled in the course have actually ran through a sales sequence yourself. You've opted into a particular lead magnet or you've opted into a particular bribe. You're presented with a compelling sales message or a sales letter and then you actually enrolled into the course. You ran through that exact same sequence. That's a little meta, but if you think back, that's exactly what's happened. And now let's look at another particular page, an important page on a lot of modern day sales funnels or modern day sale sequences, which is the content article or pre-sell page. Now, the objective here is a little advanced, but I'll explain it to you guys briefly. There is very rarely an actual CTA or call to action on a content or article pre-sell page. A lot of this stuff, the objective is to actually pixel the visitor. And what a pixel is, it's a cookie or a branding, which will tell the advertiser or mark to the advertiser that you've landed on that particular site. Later, they'll come back and remark it to you guys. And we're going to go through all that stuff, especially remarketing in that looping module. God, you guys are going to be remarketing experts by the time you complete the remarketing module. It's the most advanced in-depth remarketing course I think that anyone's ever been through. So you're going to be pros with remarketing. Remarketing means or is just a marketing methodology or tactic where if a visitor lands on a particular page and then exits or bounces that page without taking a particular action, even if they do take a particular action, we can actually continue to market to that particular person. So, for instance, you've landed on a page where I've encouraged you or I've made an offer for you to buy a particular product. You've decided not to buy it, you've left, then I can come back and follow you around the internet, provided you have included your cookies, of course, encouraging you to make the particular purchase that you decided not to. And over time, of course, I can make particular offers or present different offers like reducing the price or trying to work out why it is you didn't buy. So it feels a price issue. I may offer some kind of a discount or if I feel as though I didn't sell myself appropriately, I may create an advertisement that sells myself or sells my company or better sells the product, which of course is a three-tend and the straight line persuasion system. I don't want to get ahead of myself that you guys come for your starting. So you will probably turn and you start seeing how exactly a straight line persuasion system marries well with a straight line marketing system and why it's such an effective marketing methodology. So the content article page is a page in our sales funnel that typically exists to pixel the audience or pixel the viewer. That's not to say, of course, that it doesn't serve other purposes. Another purpose is it could create a certain amount of authority. So you could have a particular pre-sell page that really does sell the product in a way that a traditional funnel page cannot sell. And you would have a certain amount of persuasive elements on that page and you can create a really compelling offer. And even on that pre-sell page, there could be a CTA. So, for instance, we can see here on this page, for those of you whose eyesight is good enough, you can see that the ever bright smile link is actually clickable. That's a hyperlink as is the ultra white smile, I believe, another hyperlink. And that's likely to go directly to a sales page. So a pre-sell page can absolutely link towards sales page. Another advantage of an article page is, of course, you can use these article pages on particular ad networks like Tivola and Outbrain and then the other content ad networks, which we'll speak about later in our traffic module. But some, basically, some ad networks, just like we purchase traffic from Google and we purchase traffic from Facebook, some ad networks that require us to send traffic to content. Okay, so we're required to send our traffic straight to content, so straight to a page like this, as opposed to sending them straight to an opt-in page. So be mindful of that. Some ad networks really prefer and require and mandate advertisers to send their traffic directly to a page similar to this. Alternatively, a page like this can add authority and credibility. Again, a page like this can be used to pixel a particular audience for future and further retarget them. So that's another important page in our funnel. The other one is the famous VSL. Now, the VSL is exactly what it sounds like. It's a video sales letter. So we know that we've had a look at the long-form sales letter earlier, two slides ago. And the VSL accomplishes the exact same thing, but it's just a different delivery mechanism. It does it through video. And we have a VSL here of Jordan. And this particular VSL was selling, it's escaped my mind. It might have been the script builder course or the straight line persuasion course. It's actually escaped my mind. But the VSL is simply a long-form sales letter delivered through video. Often it's a white screen with a PowerPoint. It's known in the industry as an ugly VSL. And by that, what it means is it doesn't focus too much on the aesthetics. It doesn't need to look pretty. It's often more effective, but at least pretty. The uglier it is, it's just a white screen with a PowerPoint. And at least all the content that you typically or normally have, we know one-form sales letter. I like the sales letter we explored earlier. It would list the exact same content, but it would do so in video format. A lot of the times the controls will be hidden. By that, what I mean is the visitor can't actually pause the video or forward the video or rewind the video. The controls are actually hidden from the visitor. And even the buy button is hidden. So you can see there click here to add to cart. This would suggest that they're at the end of the video. That's a timed button there. So typically if the VSL lasts for 10 or 15 minutes, which is not unusual. That's a typical length for a VSL. By the ninth minute, for example, the actual click here to add to cart button will then appear. By the way, the buyer to that, the button will be hidden as will all the trust seals, the 100% money back guarantee, the secure checkout and so forth. All that will be hidden so that the visitor will feel as though they're actually watching a valuable piece of content as opposed to being presented with a sales message. So if we hide all that, if we hide the click to add to cart button, we hide all the credit cards, we hide the secure checkout and then 100% money guarantee. All those trust seals have gone. The actual click to add to cart is gone. And all you have in front of you is a screen with a particular message. You or me as a visitor are more likely to receive that message, receive it as valuable content without our guard being held up high, feeling as though we're being pushed into making a purchase. And we won't be eager either to click the add to cart button and see how much the product costs before actually being presented with a sales message, which is why we time all of those elements and then they come and switch on towards the end of the presentation after we've listed all the benefits, after we've listed the guarantee, after we've listed the price, we've gone through all those persuasive elements and then finally we'll allow the visitor to click the add to cart to find out the price to work out whether or not it's a good purchase for him or her. So that's our VSL. The final page I want to go through in any typical funnel is the webinar sign up page. Now webinars are especially popular right now. They're a trending method for generating sales and revenue and we do them with almost all of our clients. Of course it depends on the product and market. Some markets are just very much webinar reverse. But ultimately webinars are a great way of monetizing your list and we're going to go through all those details in the future in this webinar actually. Excuse me in this class, in this module. But webinars are another very important sales page in our particular funnel. And the objective here is just to get a sign up. Okay, so we want to that's how we're going to sell. So rather than in this particular case or in the case of a webinar, we're not going to actually be selling our particular product via a traditional sales letter. What we're going to do is do most of our selling in a webinar or through the webinar. And then people, visitors or attendees of the webinar, once the webinar has concluded, they'll be made in particular offer in some cases, in some cases they won't. But in most cases, a visitor is actually being made an offer at the end of the webinar where they will be presented with an option or opportunity to buy a particular product. This is one of the webinars that Jordan had run recently. You can see the basic elements there. There's headline, there's a registration button. At the bottom, there are of course the benefits to attending the webinar, which is not unlike the opt-in page. If you remember, we had a headline. We had the benefits for signing up, requesting access to the interview for the opt-in page. The same objective exists here for our webinar page. We have a headline, a registration button, we have the benefits for attending the webinar, and then the option of course to reserve your seat and get started. So we're just as simple as that. They're our major web pages. Get familiar with them. Understand when we use each one, why we will choose each one. Understand the terminology. And I guarantee you this will be the most difficult module because you're really going to be exposed to concepts and terms that you might not be familiar with or might have been exposed to within the past. Hang in there. It'll only get easier and only get more exciting. But that's our basic funnel pages. Alright guys, now that you have a basic understanding of what forms a regular sales sequence or sales funnel and you know all the major and most important pages, let's carry on with our basic internet marketing terminology. The first term I'd like to introduce you guys to, for those of you who aren't familiar with it, is affiliate marketing. Now affiliate marketing functions almost as your virtual sales team. So provided you are actually a vendor as opposed to an affiliate marketer. Now let me explain the difference. Let's take the analogy that you're an affiliate marketer. So as an affiliate marketer, you don't own a product. You don't have a business. You have nothing to sell. But you may be very good at buying traffic and we're going to talk about the best ways to buy traffic and you guys are all going to be great traffic buyers by the end of the course. But right now, say hypothetically, you already have those skills. You know how to buy traffic, but you don't have anything to send the traffic to so you don't have an offer. Now that's when affiliate marketing comes in. So affiliate marketing is an opportunity for you to take somebody else's product, take somebody else's service or offering and send traffic to it and you will then be reunited or you will receive some kind of a commission for successfully selling that product. Now that's in the case of course affiliate marketing as opposed to CPA marketing or cost per action marketing where rather than having to actually make a sale before you enjoy a commission or can claim a commission. You can actually claim that same commission by simply encouraging the visitor to take any kind of particular action. For example, it could be as simple as leaving an email address and converting a prospect into a lead that could actually warrant a commission. Alternatively, you could send traffic to a free offer that's popular. For example, in the supplement space, you can have a free trial of a particular supplement and having successfully encouraged the visitor or the prospect to actually take advantage of the free offer will then entitle you as an affiliate or CPA marketer to claim a commission. Now, I think it's better understood by actually exploring the networks. So an affiliate network is a marketplace. So it's just a collection or an aggregation of different offers and what you would do then is you would go into a category that interests you, you would pick a particular offer, the network would then give you a direct link. That direct link is just yours. It's been generated just for you. It's entirely unique. You would then buy traffic, send it to that particular URL and if the traffic that you purchase runs through a sequence and makes a purchase or buys the product that you're attempting to promote, then you will then be able to claim a commission from the network. So the network is the most popular digital marketplace right now is ClickBank and we'll go through ClickBank in a moment just to show you guys around. You would then be paid a commission by ClickBank and ClickBank will then take a commission for itself for having married up you and the vendor and the remaining funds or the remaining amount of the sale will then be passed on to the vendor. Who is the business that actually had gotten that offer or created that offer or product and then placed it on that network. It may sound a little convoluted now, but we'll go through the affiliate network so you guys can familiarize yourself with it. And once we actually play around in the network, you'll understand how simple it is and it's really, really, it's a great research tool and I encourage you all to get familiar with affiliate marketing just so you can know what's out there in the marketplace. You can find out what's selling best and also you may want to test the marketplace. You may want to determine whether or not there's an appetite for a particular product that you've created or service that you may have created. So you may want to become an affiliate marketer for a week or two, promote a couple of products and see whether or not the conversion rate is high enough for you to actually create your own product that's similar to the one that you're promoting. So that's a popular use for affiliate marketing and one I encourage, especially for competitive intelligence, gathering as much information about the marketplace and about your customer or prospect as you can. But what I warn you against and what I recommend strongly against is actually becoming a full-time affiliate marketer, which is a popular avenue right now. And I've seen a lot of people do well. I've seen a lot of short wins. And by short wins, I mean I've seen people do well in the space of six to 12 months, very most 18 months. I haven't yet seen anybody actually make a or create a successful, sustainable long-term business doing nothing but affiliate marketing themselves. So by that I mean promoting offers that belong to somebody else or somebody else's offering as opposed to, of course, creating a product and then you yourself putting it onto the affiliate network, then you would function as a vendor and then other people will be promoting your product. And I would encourage you guys to do that if you've created a product and can actually get it onto an affiliate network, which isn't challenging. You guys can play around the network and we'll play have a play together around the network so you can familiarize yourself with the interface and how to go about functioning both as a vendor and affiliate marketer. I would encourage you guys to use affiliate marketing or make use of it long term as a vendor. So what's the wait for you to attract risk free traffic? Because you're only paying of course for the traffic once it actually converts into a sale as opposed to actually buying traffic from other networks like Facebook and Google and so forth, where you actually have to pay for clicks or pay CPM pay for impressions, but whether or not it converts. So it is a great risk free traffic source, but not a long term, long term sustainable business model if you want to function exclusively as affiliate marketer. So affiliate marketing, nothing wrong with it. I absolutely support it, but I support it only of course for market research, for competitive intelligence for functioning or while functioning as a vendors selling your products on the affiliate network as opposed to of course, promoting other people's products. Alright guys as promised we're going to have a quick look around the clickbank marketplace now. We're at www.clickbank.com and here we are at the affiliate marketplace and as you all know by now an affiliate marketplace is simply a platform that aggregates different offers by different vendors and encourages affiliate marketers to actually promote those particular offers. So let's take a look here at the affiliate marketplace again, we're at www.clickbank.com, we're on the navigation bar, we're going to click affiliate marketplace and then we're going to look at the categories and find the category that interests us. Either we're in that space now or we want to do a little bit of market research or gather some competitive intelligence in that particular market. So let's take a look at health and fitness. Alright hypothetically we want to move into this space and we're on clickbank, so clickbank is all digital products. I know they're making a recent move encouraging the promotion of physical products but right now they are famously a digital marketplace. Okay so let's navigate or excuse me filter by gravity. Now the way clickbank is set in their infinite wisdom they've created a special algorithm that they've termed or titled gravity and it is basically the most popular and highest converting and the kind of offers that most people want to promote is not actually under popularity as you would expect it to be but it's actually a digital actually listed under gravity so the higher gravity the more popular the actual offering is so the more people who want to promote it and the reason people want to promote a product more aggressively is almost always because it is a higher converting offer. So let's sort by gravity now let's take a look at the highest gravity offer here which is fat diminisher it is a conversion monster $10,000 prizes in January so what this means is this particular vendor is prepared to pay his most successful affiliate market as $10,000 prize in addition of course to whatever commission they earn in selling the product. Now for those of you who want to delve deeper into affiliate marketing against my advice so what I do recommend is you not promote products that have such a high gravity like 302.1 because no matter what channel or platform you attempt to actually serve ads on the market would have been inundated with this particular offer 302.1 gravity far too many people have seen it. It won't convert well not for you I think it's had its time frankly it may have been a really high converter at some point but I certainly wouldn't recommend or advocate you're picking an offer like this to promote but for our intents and purposes let's take a look at it. Let's take a look at the few key metrics the important stuff some of the stuff we've already explored so here we've got conversions are over 8% that means for all the people that you're actually going to be sending to this particular page this vendor argues or suggests that 8% of them which is a little too high in my opinion but let's take his word at face value. 8% of them will actually convert into buyers and there's a 2.15 EPC so $2.15 for everybody who actually clicks excuse me $2.15 per click so if it clicked on a cost us $1 we can expect to earn $2.15 which means we've got a profit of $1.15 which is why you can see it's potentially a very lucrative product to promote because it has such a high earning per click and that's from cold and warm. The initial sale is $36.50 that's the amount of money that you can typically expect to elicit from the customer at point of sale hypothetically then we're going to be issued with a direct link of course you would need to first create a home page you'd go to the home page and then you would actually create an account with click bank and let me just do that with you guys. Alright so we want to click here we're on the home page now I'm going to click create a link or create an account excuse me you're going to input your details then you're going to submit you're going to give them your banking information or if you prefer to be paid by check you give them your personal information and make that click then and then you'll be issued with a user name and password. Now that you have an account all you have to do is click this promote button and then you'll be sent a direct URL link which is a unique link that's just for you nobody else has it and you'll be sending traffic to that particular link and every time somebody purchases a product after having gone through your link to lend or end up on that sales funnel the vendor sales funnel you'll be paid a commission. So at point of sale you can expect to make $36.50 and there's an average rebuild of $47.21 what that means is there's a back end so there's some continuity there it's likely a monthly type forced continuity encouraging people to pay every month for access to whatever it is they're selling. Average rebuild of 50% that means 50% of the people who take you up on the initial point of sale of the initial product excuse me was just $36.50 at point of sale 50% of them will actually buy the second product or the or take advantage of the monthly back end the continuity whatever it may be a little difficult to say without finding the offer finding out with the office all of that excuse me so here we are on a VSL hi I'm what likely to make a lot of noise okay that's another point that we didn't know. And then we mentioned VSL's typically are on autoplay which means that the video will start playing before the visitor decides to click a play button now autoplay almost always increases conversions but you just need to be mindful of some networks really don't like autoplay and they can actually penalize advertisers for sending traffic to an autoplay. The most popular of course is Google Google hates it don't bother if you're going to advertise on Google stay away from autoplay you'll just find yourself getting in a whole lot of trouble in the account getting banned that's if your pages were even approved in the first place but in this particular case the advertiser decided to go with autoplay they're on a typical ugly VSL that we spoke about earlier not very aesthetically pleasing at least not the video the the imagery on the side is a little different and not not a bad design but like you can see here there's nothing to do this. Lead. No need to spend too much time on leads a lead of course is a prospect who you have successfully converted through your opt in page that is they've entered their name and then they've entered an email address so quite simply you've sent traffic to an opt in page. The visitor has gone from prospect to lead by submitting their information they haven't yet of course made a purchase nice and simple email lists now email list function as the lifeblood of your business meaning at no email list no business quite frankly and this the fastest easiest way to make money online right now is by the collection and gathering of as much or as many excuse me email lists as possible click the email address click them in or through different CRM systems and all different email marketing platforms. We recommend a few and we'll go through them in greater detail as we progress especially in the email marketing module of course but there are solutions such as get response a web active campaign and we'll go through them will go through our recommendations but right now know that. Of course once you convert a prospect into a lead now you have their email address because they've gone through that page in order to access the content that we spoke about earlier in order to access your bait they were forced to leave their name in many cases but in all cases their email address so now you have that email address. Guard it is the life force of the lifeblood of your business be mindful of the significance and importance of the email email list we're going to make great use of them going forward. Conversions conversions quite simply are a percentage of people who have taken any kind of particular action so for instance if our goal was to convert prospects into leads that is we want to convert a visitor who's landed on our landing page in two weeks. We want to convert them into a lead which means they would actually have to have opted in in that case that's the action that's the goal the opt in and for instance we've had we've sent 100 visitors we've paid for 100 clicks let's use a nice nice traffic channel so we've purchased 100 Facebook clicks we've gone on to Facebook we've purchased 100 clicks. To our opt in page and the objective of course was to convert that visitor into a lead so of that 100 say 15 people actually landed on excuse me converted which means 15 people actually left their email address our conversion rate would be 15% on that opt in page so that opt in page is said to have a 15% conversion rate. Okay so 15% of the visitors converted from prospect to lead as an example of a conversion conversion happen all over the place they don't only happen on our major funnels conversions can be tracked to be micro conversions for instance we have one particular client who makes a lot of money through their live chat functionality on their website. By that I mean they have a website and they're a training organization or a college and they have live chat functionality and a lot of the times prospective students will be chatting to course advisors through the live chat function of the website and they want to test that treat that excuse me as a goal so they wanted to test the amount of people who actually took advantage of the live chat functionality and chat to a course advisor so every time somebody would actually go on to the live chat and actually have a conversation with the course advisor about enrolling into a particular course we treated that as a goal and that therefore we actually tracked live chat activity as a conversion so if 100 people landed on the web page and only 12 people for instance committed or took advantage of the live chat and had a conversation with the course advisor we would say that 12% live chat conversion so that is conversions upsells downsells now upsells downsells are a way to increase the lifetime value of every single visitor or customer so for instance if somebody makes a purchase they have gone through our opt in page they've made a purchase we've made them an offer right away and they've committed to it and they bought the product we don't want to just simply let them go what we want to do is make them another offer so that means that the customer is now worth more to us than they would have been worth if we hadn't made them that upsell which of course means that we can spend more to acquire that customer so let's think about it practically say hypothetically we spent $10 in acquiring one particular customer who purchased a product for $7 would that be worth it all that face value it wouldn't be worth it because we had paid $10 and the customer only had given us or the customer had only made a $7 purchase excuse me which means that we're in the red $3 so it would have been a bad buy now say hypothetically after they had purchased that $7 product we made them an immediate upsell or ideally a one click upsell to increase conversions for another product valued at $5 so now they purchased a $7 product they will make another offer so right now that customer is worth $12 to us so if we paid $10 to acquire that customer and they're now worth $12 wasn't worth it you bet so it's a $2 profit and that's that point of sale now of course we all know the value right now of collecting email addresses and why that's so important is that customer may have purchased that $7 product at the point of sale right and they could decide to turn us down on the upsell now they're only worth $7 we paid $10 to them so we're in the red $3 but we have their email address and now that we have their email address we can continue to market to them and give them give them different offers or present different offers to them through their email so that $7 customer may be sent an email the one or two days later making an offer for a five or six or $7 product and then we noticed that that value of the customer has really been a big deal and that's why they've been using it for over time so they're no longer worth $7 to us but they're actually worth $12 $15 $20 because we have the ability and the capability to continue to market to them and increase the value of that client the other side is down so say we've made an offer that they've purchased or even they've declined to purchase in this case let's say that so they've declined to purchase they've opted in we've made an offer that declined to purchase we can simply let them go and say that it's a dead lead or we can remarket to them through the email marketing alternatively we can make a direct down so at point of sale meaning these this particular customer has come in they've said no they don't want to buy and then we come in with a down so a down so it could be a discount of your particular core offer which I recommend against kind of cheapens your offering or alternatively what the down so what the down so could be could be for instance a simpler product a more stripped product so you may have a an awesome product with a whole bunch of bonuses and something really fantastic and then it may be in a price point that's a little bit above what your visitor is comfortable with so what we would say is okay you may not be interested in this have a restrict some of the bonuses back we offer you something that's a little more raw or crude but at a much lower price point and that would be a down so and again that down so has increased the value of the customer to us and as long as the lifetime value of our customer is high enough we can continue to spend a lot more in acquiring each customer and that's the secret you want to be able to spend as much as you can in acquiring as many customers as you can I mean that's the secret to success you want to be able to spend more than your competitors in acquiring customers if you're able to spend more than your competitors in acquiring customer nothing's going to stop you nothing can stop you your competitors can't stop you because if you can spend $10 and acquiring a customer because you have a whole series of upsells and downsells and you're continuing to remark it to them through remarketing or retargeting as well as the through a sophisticated email funnel that continues to market to now prospects or excuse me continues to market to market excuse me to leads and previous customers then your customers can be worth a whole lot more to you than your competitors customers is worth to them which means you can spend a whole lot more in acquiring them and that's really the secret to success online because frankly anybody can buy traffic I mean traffic becomes easy to buy we're going to teach you how to buy it and the hacks or the old little tricks on being able to buy traffic a little bit more effectively than that more effective excuse me than your competitors they're disappearing slowly but surely traffic has become commoditized so the only way you're going to be able to outwork your competitors is to spend more on traffic than they are and the only way that's going to happen of course is if your customers are worth more to you than their customers are worth to them the next definition is impressions now impression is whenever your ad verse a for instance you're buying media let's talk about banner ads so you can purchase a series of banner ads on a particular website so we're going to manage placement this time just to make it nice and simple you're going on to a website say for instance Fox News you go into Fox News and you want to buy a banner right at the top of Fox News every time your ad is served or loaded onto that particular page that counts as an impression that's one impression now if you're actually buying banner ads on a CPM basis or how many CPM basis just means basically that every time or every 1000 times your ad shows you're going to pay it a particular particular amount okay so there's going to be a pre-determined or agreed rate where you're going to pay a certain amount of money for every time your ad is loaded 1000 times on the site that's CPM now if your ad appears above the fold that means that well before we go into that actually I'm a little concerned into my Twitter or not I should explain the impressions before I explain above and below the fold but let's do this explain above and below the fold and then we'll come back to impressions and make a little bit more sense now above the fold means that once you actually land on a website if you don't have to scroll down everything you see in front of you without having to touch your cursor or touch the arrow that is considered above the fold now if you have to scroll down scroll down anything anything you scroll down to is considered below the fold okay so if you look at any kind of if you just browse any kind of particular website any website you like and you don't touch your cursor and you don't scroll down what you see in front of you is above the fold okay once you start scrolling down anything that was not visible prior to scrolling but is now visible after having scrolled is below the fold now when we talk about impressions what we're saying is that that particular advertisement that you've purchased that banner ad that display ad that you purchase that you've gone ahead and put on Fox News dot com every time that's loaded onto the site that's counted as a single impression and you're paying for that for impressions on a 1000 impression basis so what every time that every time that ad is served excuse me every 1000 times that ad is served you're going to pay a pre determined and agreed rate now be mindful of the fact that even if the ad is served below the fold that still counts as an impression and a lot of people don't know this and a lot of people teach this and they teach it incorrectly and they ought to know better but every time a particular ad is served or loaded on a page as counted as an impression that's why if you go on to a media buying platform and we'll have a look at some of them in our traffic module you'll notice that placements below the fold are significantly cheaper than placements above the fold because if it is placed below the fold there is no guarantee and no telling whether or not the visitor decides to scroll down low enough to see your ad so even though your ad has a low profile it may not have been viewed by the visitor and if you're paying on a CPM basis it's going to cost you money if however you decide or you're afforded the opportunity of paying on a CPC basis which means that you only pay money once somebody clicks your ad then it becomes less significant or less important because you're only going to pay once somebody clicks the ad so whether or not it's above the fold or below the fold becomes much less relevant so just be mindful of that and we'll explore this in greater detail of course in our traffic module but I just wanted to make that clear to you guys above the fold, below the fold impressions now traffic sources, now traffic sources are nice and simple basically it is the people who visit your website so simple as that they can be organic or they can be purchased organic traffic is SEO traffic, search engine optimised traffic primarily now there's often a debate between organic versus purchased I'm a strong believer in predictability which is why I advocate purchase traffic over organic traffic in almost every circumstance organic traffic is great for a brand building when you want to build awareness you want to be able to appear to be often bigger than you are and organic traffic really helps in that pursuit but primarily purchase traffic is going to be the fastest, most predictable way of growing building and scaling any business, organic traffic isn't free contrary to your common opinion organic traffic still costs money because it's going to take a lot of time to actually get your direct links I know a lot of people are proponents of PBNs which I'm not and which is why I'm not going to be teaching you PBNs or private blog networks and what that is basically is a collection of different websites that an owner or a PBN owner would have created or purchased and then they would use those websites to link directly to a site that they're trying to rank and all those direct backlinks give the website ostensibly a bit of Google juice and they allow for better rankings it's a bit black hat I don't support it if you're going to go down the organic route which again I'm not a stronger proponent of and I don't really think that it's something that you guys should focus too much of your attention on the future of the internet is purchase traffic that you actually pay money for that's simply the future it's the way the internet is heading SEO hacks are slowly becoming a thing of the past but if you're really committed to organic traffic and SEO I would spend or invest a lot of time in in citations in great content fantastic brilliant content that gets natural regular normal link activity so that people are actually writing about you and linking directly to you as opposed to force links and of course citation and directory networks are something that I would advocate outside of that by your traffic by your traffic send it to sales funnels and you'll be better prepared to succeed online you'll be better and your success will be predictable you'll know exactly how much money you're going to make you'll know exactly what a customer is worth to you how much you're prepared to make excuse me prepared to spend to acquire a customer it just becomes a more sustainable and predictable business model and it's why we certainly do in this course and as I would encourage all of my clients to think the same kind of way purchase traffic or buy it or purchase media is certainly the future and organic traffic is something that should happen just that organically as opposed to being forced alright guys congratulations you've successfully gone through all the basic internet marketing terminology if you know all of what we've discussed you're in good stead to excel and succeed in the straight line marketing system and I have every bit of confidence that you'll do just that now it's time to move on to our advanced internet marketing terminology and concepts now like I said in the introduction just because we're calling it advanced it does not mean it's optional it's absolutely mandatory but it's just taking a step up or level up from the terms that we've had that we've excuse me explored a moment ago let's take a look click through rates now click through rates simply are a ratio that compares the number of visitors which senior advertisement and we compare that of course against the number of people click that excuse me advertisement so a click through rate functions as a metric that allows us to determine how well our ad is actually performing so if I've got an ad on a ad let's take a look at for example at search network so say hypothetically I'm on Google I purchased some ads on Google on the Google search network as opposed to the display network which will explain in the traffic module but I purchased some I purchased an ad on the search network and somebody searching for carpet cleaners they've made that search and my ad has shown up every time my ad shows up that's going to count as one impression okay and every time somebody clicks that ad that's going to count we're going to treat that as a click so a click through rate is the number of people who have seen the ad compared against the number of people who have clicked the ad so the simplest way to explain it is say hypothetically I've purchased ads for carpet cleaners somebody search for carpet cleaner and our ads come up every 100 people who have searched for carpet cleaners and then have seen our search ad that encourages people to click on the ad for whatever our opt-in but every time somebody clicks that ad so they've seen it a hundred times have clicked it five times our click through rate is 5% so as simple as that it's a ratio that compares the number of visitors who have seen the ad compared against the number of people who have actually clicked the ad so we have a lot of reasons to increase our click through rate the most common reason right now especially with Google is your quality score the higher your CTR Google will treat that as the more relevant and enticing your ad is which means that increases your quality score which of course reduces the amount of money you have to pay to acquire the traffic now we'll explain all that in detail in our traffic module but for now just know that your click through rate is the number of people who have clicked your ad as compared against the number of people who have seen it raw clicks vs unique clicks now this is a particularly interesting concept because a lot of people don't know the difference and it makes a huge difference or it provides an enormous amount of skewing it will excuse me skew your data if you actually count raw clicks as unique clicks or the other way around now a unique click is someone who has clicked on your ad once in the last 24 hours that is what we consider a unique click now a raw click is the number of times your ad has been clicked period so for instance if I'm clicking in my ad and I've clicked it once and then I've decided to hit the back button and then click it again and I've clicked it again now all of a sudden you've got three clicks but really you've only got one unique click a unique click is actually measured against your IP every 24 hours so for instance if I was to see that ad that I liked and I had clicked it and then I reversed or I went backwards and then I came back left my computer and browser on and I came back 25 hours later and I clicked that same ad and we'd have two unique clicks okay so we have two unique clicks and we'd also have two raw clicks just be mindful of that especially when you're trying to determine your EPC or your earnings per click which of course is how much money you make every time somebody clicks your ad and that's very useful information because you're actually going to quite often buy traffic on a cost per click basis so you want to know how much money you earn per click if you're going to pay cost per click so when you determine your EPC it's important that you only consider your unique clicks so you can get that true and accurate data as opposed to of course counting your raw clicks which is very skewed data it's corrupted data and it doesn't really matter okay so especially if you're in a dirty industry which we've played in we have clients who are operating in pretty dirty industries where competitors actively try to click on ads that they know that our clients are excuse me paying on a cost per click basis and they're constantly clicking on that same ad over and over again there are security systems in some of the more matured networks that actually prevent that kind of behavior but it does skew our own internal data and we look at raw clicks and compare that against our EPC or our own for a click and we look at your unique clicks how many times a particular visitor with one IP address will click on your ad within a 24 hour period that's what's most important the next concept is direct and affiliate links we had a look at affiliate marketing and we spoke about direct links very briefly but basically a direct link is the link that's generated by the network so we've had a look at the click bank network and that we were sent a particular link where we could actually send traffic to in order to promote a particular offer that we had selected on that network or on that platform that link that you were sent by the network is your direct link you're the only person with that link it is entirely unique nobody else on earth has that link it's just yours and you send traffic to that particular link and it converts into a sale then of course you'll be rewarded with a commission in the case of affiliate marketing now in the case of CPA marketing or through a if you want to promote a product through a CPA network what would happen is you'll be sent a direct link also but rather than having to actually complete a sale you all you would have to do is complete a particular goal or objective quite often it's a little more than having the visitor that you've attracted simply put their name and email address into an opt-in box or take advantage of a free offer or trial or something of a like tracking links now tracking links will take a look at how to actually generate a traffic link but a tracking link is a special link that you yourself would have created through your own systems your own tools and we'll talk about how we're going to go about doing that and the value of a tracking link is of course to track the performance of a particular campaign or traffic source so for instance if I was to generate a traffic link exclusively to test the performance of a solo ad and the solo ad simply is an advertisement that goes out to a rented email list so you may for instance you may own an email list because you guys are all going to be pro internet marketers by the end of this course and you know the important and value in collecting data like we've explained so you may have collected a group a significant email list of say 10,000 names or emails in the health and wellness space so you've collected all that data now what I would do as an advertiser is I would go to somebody like you with a with a very relevant and useful list to my offer because I myself hypothetically am promoting or selling a health and wellness offer I would go to you and I would ask could I send your list an offer or could I send an email to your list encouraging them to make a purchase of my particular product or service okay and if you were to agree I would send you the ad copy or the actual what I would expect you to send in the email and as long as the link of course into my sales funnel and that is called a solo ad so I'll actually ask you to send me an email to go ahead and email your list on my behalf to sell my product and in exchange for that of course we I would send you a certain amount of a way to be in a great rate whether it's on a cost per click basis everybody clicks the link I'll pay you or I'll just pay you on a green fixed fee for sending the email out to your entire list whatever it might be we'll talk about the strategies later in traffic but I want to now know how well that ad performed now you're not the only person I've approached I may have approached three or four different people always similar list size or promoting the same offer my offer of course and I've sent the same ad copy to all of the list owners that I've decided to rent a list from now there's no way for me to tell which email this work best you may have a great list somebody else may have an even better list and there's no way for me to track the performance of each list so the only way I'm going to be able to do that effectively is if I send you a tracking link as opposed to sending you my direct URL so rather than sending you a URL to my sales funnel I'm going to be sending you a tracking link and I'm going to which is a unique URL that's going to redirect to my sales funnel and I'm also going to send another tracking link which is another direct URL to my sales funnel to another solo ad provider and I'll do the same to the third and then once I've collected all the data I'm able to have a look at my system and look at my back end and determine which ad placement served me best or excuse me which list produced the best results and I'll look at the conversions like we spoke about earlier so I'd look at the number of clicks I'd look at from the clicks the number of people have actually opted in or the number of people have actually made a purchase and I'll be able to compare the quality of the list as a solo ad provider against the others and that can only be done effectively through tracking links and we're going to have a look at different tracking links how to generate them and we're going to be looking at the actual platforms that we use to track the links and then take a look at the performance of each link or each traffic source. Now split tests are another important tool. We're going to talk about how to perform split tests and the value of split tests but basically a split test is simply looking at a single ad variable and determining whether or not a change resulted in a bump or rising conversion so hypothetically I could have a opt-in page that said something like Jordan Belfort the Wolf of Wall Street find out his secrets to make sure that the link is not a mistake. So that's if success and wealth by submitting your email address below and then I could have an alternative page that says something as simple as Jordan Belfort's secrets click below. So all we've done is changed the copy and changed the headline. Now that may have a serious and significant impact on our conversions. Of course in this case when I say conversions I mean the amount of people who have submitted their name and email address into the opt-in form to take advantage or to gain access to the baked content. So that was promised in the opt-in form. So by simply changing the words on the page I may have increased or changed the conversion. So I want to run a split test to determine which of the two variables which of the two headline changes or which of the two pieces of copy converted at a higher rate. And of course I'm going to keep the winner and cut the loser and you carry on with that process and you can split test just about any element you can think of whether it's copy which of course means the actual words we used or it's the color and layout of the actual web page. It could be where we put particular elements whether we put the opt-in box above the fold or below the fold or whether we include trust seals like 100% guarantee or list the different kind of credit card providers that we accept and all these different elements. It can be split tested and the objective of course is to increase our conversion rate by changing as many elements and comparing as many variables as we can and we're going to go through that a little later in the course. And the last and one of the most important terms that we're going to explore now is frequency caps. Now a frequency cap simply is the amount of times a visitor will see your advertisement. Now we recommended that three to five so we wouldn't want a visitor to see our ad any more than three or five times. If you're buying out on a CPM basis which of course is you pay every time your ad gets served 1000 times then frequency cap is everything. It's completely it's absolutely important because we don't want somebody seeing our ad seven or eight times when we know that they're not interested. We're paying for every time that that person sees the ad and if they haven't clicked on the ad. So in this case we're talking about buying media or buying display ads display ads if they haven't clicked on the ad after the third fourth fifth time we're pretty confident they're not going to be clicking on our ads. So we don't want them to see our ad another 10 15 20 times it's it's frankly a waste of money and we're paying we're paying on a CPM basis we're paying every time people see our ad as opposed to every time they click our ad. Now if we're paying or purchasing ads on a CPC basis or every time they clicked on an ad that would be a different story. We wouldn't be as concerned with frequency caps because if they're not clicking we're not paying but if we're paying as we commonly do for display ads on a CPM basis every time our ads being viewed or every thousand times our ads been loaded onto a site and we know somebody's already seen our ad five times and haven't clicked. We sure as hell don't want them to keep seeing it we sure as hell don't want to keep paying for that which is why we have to turn our frequency cap on and typically we recommend I strongly recommend never going above a five if you're again if you're paying on a CPC basis do whatever you like it's not it's largely irrelevant your frequency cap if you're paying on CPM don't ever go over five it's very rarely if ever I would encourage or I'll try to purchase traffic on a frequency cap of five to ten because I just know if they've seen the ad that many times unless something significant has happened I would encourage you to keep your cap at about five at the very most ideally about three if somebody hasn't seen or clicked on your ad three times there's absolutely no reason for you to keep running or serving that add to that particular person if you're paying every time it's loaded.
